description_long: This object is a highly detailed representation of a mature coconut palm tree, featuring a long, slender, and slightly curved trunk with a rough, ringed texture. At the top, a dense cluster of large, feathery fronds radiates outward, displaying a mix of green and yellowish hues, with some fronds appearing older and more faded. Several coconuts are visible nestled among the fronds, their round, fibrous husks hanging in small groups. The trunk is dark brown with lighter, segmented bands, and the fronds exhibit a natural, organic spread with some drooping and others extending horizontally. Two long, thin support lines or ropes are attached to the upper part of the tree, suggesting stabilization or anchoring, possibly for urban landscaping. The overall appearance is lifelike, with attention to the natural asymmetry and texture of a real coconut palm.
description: A lifelike coconut palm tree with a curved, ringed trunk, lush green fronds, and visible coconuts clustered at the top, stabilized by long support lines.
description_view_0: The side view highlights the full curve of the trunk, the spread of the fronds, and the attached support lines extending outward.
description_view_1: The top-down view emphasizes the radial arrangement of the fronds and the central cluster of coconuts, with support lines visible from above.
description_view_2: This angled side view shows the trunk's curvature, the layered fronds, and the coconuts nestled among the leaves, with support lines extending diagonally.
description_view_3: The front view presents the trunk rising vertically with fronds fanning out symmetrically, and the support lines extending to the right.
attribute_husk_pattern: The coconut husks visible among the fronds have a coarse, fibrous texture with a dense arrangement of longitudinal fibers running from the base to the tip. The fibers appear irregular and slightly matted, with some areas showing more pronounced ridges and others smoother patches. The coloration varies from light brown to darker brown, with subtle streaks and natural markings.
attribute_stem_attachment: The coconuts are visibly attached to the palm by short, thick stems that connect them to the base of the fronds. The attachment points are clustered near the crown of the tree, with the stems appearing robust and slightly curved.
attribute_growth_cluster: The coconuts are part of a cluster, with at least three visible coconuts grouped closely together among the fronds.
attribute_proximity_to_palm_trees: The coconuts are directly attached to the palm tree, growing at the crown where the fronds emerge.
